qa_pairs.question: What are the best practices for configuring and running GPU jobs on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: When configuring GPU jobs on Trillium, it is recommended to avoid using the `--mem` option because memory is fixed per GPU (192 GB) or per node (768 GB). Always specify the node count and use `--gpus-per-node=4` for whole-node or multi-node jobs. Users should load only the modules they need, be explicit with software versions for reproducibility (e.g., `cuda/12.6`), test on a single GPU before scaling to multiple GPUs or nodes, and monitor usage with `nvidia-smi` to ensure GPUs are fully utilized.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor the status and performance of their jobs on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor the status and performance of their jobs on Trillium using several SLURM commands. `squeue` shows all jobs in the queue, or `squeue -u $USER` to view only personal jobs. `squeue -j JOBID` or `scontrol show job JOBID` provides detailed information for a specific job. `squeue --start -j JOBID` offers a rough estimate of when a pending job is expected to start, though this estimate can be inaccurate. To cancel a job, use `scancel JOBID`. For a live snapshot of CPU and memory usage of a running job, use `jobperf JOBID`. Information about past jobs, including start time, run time, node usage, and exit status, can be viewed with `sacct` or through the my.SciNet portal, which saves performance data collected every two minutes.
